description: The DNS processing mode in NetworkManager describes how DNS is processed on the system. Depending the mode some changes the system's DNS may not be respected.
rationale: To ensure that DNS resolver settings are respected, a DNS mode in NetworkManager must be configured.
ocil_clause: the dns key under main does not exist or is not set to "none" or "default"
ocil:
Verify that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 has a DNS mode configured in Network Manager.

$ NetworkManager --print-config
[main]
dns=<sub idref="var_networkmanager_dns_mode" />

stig.checktext:
[main]
dns=none

If the dns key under main does not exist or is not set to "none" or "default", this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure NetworkManager in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to use a DNS mode.

In "/etc/NetworkManager/NetworkManager.conf" add the following line in the "[main]" section:

dns = none
stig.srg_requirement: {{ full_name }} must configure a DNS processing mode set be Network Manager.

title: NetworkManager DNS Mode Must Be Must Configured
